perturbation.original_text: In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual and non-perpetual license (subject to the duration for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A5 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), except that for Titles listed in Schedule A1-A2 of Schedule A, Licensor can only grant Licensee distribution rights to up to six (6) MSOs plus two (2) of China's Internet TV license holders or their OTT Internet- based video partners by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD).
perturbation.changed_text: In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee an exclusive, royalty-bearing, perpetual license (subject to the duration for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A5 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), and for Titles listed in Schedule A1-A2 of Schedule A, Licensor grants Licensee exclusive distribution rights to all MSOs and all of China's Internet TV license holders or their OTT Internet-based video partners by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD).
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' and 'royalty-free' to 'royalty-bearing,' the nature of the license is fundamentally altered. Additionally, the modified text grants Licensee distribution rights to 'all MSOs' and 'all of China's Internet TV license holders,' removing the restrictions mentioned in the original text. The 'except' clause from the original text is removed, directly contradicting the distribution limitations. This creates an in-text contradiction since the original section limits the distribution to a specific number of MSOs, now the modification makes this section grant distribution rights to all of them. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the true scope of the distribution rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: License Grant. In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual and non-perpetual license (subject to the duration for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A5 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), except that for Titles listed in Schedule A1-A2 of Schedule A, Licensor can only grant Licensee distribution rights to up to six (6) MSOs plus two (2) of China's Internet TV license holders or their OTT Internet- based video partners by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD). China's current Internet TV license holders include: CNTV (中国网络电视台/未来电视), BesTV (百视通), Wasu (华数), Southern Media Cooperation (南方传媒), Hunan TV (芒果TV), China National Radio/Galaxy Internet TV (GITV) (银河电视), and China Radio International (中国国际广播电台).
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text specify that the license granted to Licensee is 'non-exclusive' and allows distribution rights to a limited number of MSOs and China's Internet TV license holders ('up to six (6) MSOs plus two (2) of China's Internet TV license holders'). The changed text alters this by granting an 'exclusive' license with distribution rights to 'all MSOs' and 'all of China's Internet TV license holders.' This creates a direct contradiction because the original text limits distribution, while the changed text removes these limitations, resulting in uncertainty about the actual scope of the distribution rights. This adheres to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The consideration for the licenses granted by Licensor to Licensee under this Agreement is the issuance of the IP Common Shares as defined in the Securities Purchase Agreement, dated as of November 23, 2015, by and among the Licensee and the Licensor (the "Share Consideration").
perturbation.changed_text: The consideration for the licenses granted by Licensor to Licensee under this Agreement is a combination of the issuance of the IP Common Shares as defined in the Securities Purchase Agreement, dated as of November 23, 2015, by and among the Licensee and the Licensor (the "Share Consideration") AND a royalty of 15% of all gross revenue generated by Licensee from the Titles.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defines the 'Share Consideration' as the sole consideration for the licenses. The modification adds 'AND a royalty of 15% of all gross revenue generated by Licensee from the Titles,' introducing a new financial obligation (royalty payment) that directly contradicts the original provision that states the Share Consideration is the only consideration. By stating this in section 10 and contradicting it in the same section, creates uncertainty about the true consideration required under the agreement, the original sections clearly states one thing and it is contradicted later in the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consideration. No royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed by Licensee under this Agreement. The consideration for the licenses granted by Licensor to Licensee under this Agreement is the issuance of the IP Common Shares as defined in the Securities Purchase Agreement, dated as of November 23, 2015, by and among the Licensee and the Licensor (the "Share Consideration").
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Share Consideration is the *sole* consideration for the licenses. The changed text adds a royalty payment *in addition* to the Share Consideration. This directly contradicts the original statement and the 'Consideration' section, which explicitly states 'No royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed by Licensee under this Agreement.' The introduction of royalty creates a clear and irreconcilable inconsistency regarding the financial obligations of the Licensee, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Reports and all information contained in the Reports shall be considered Confidential Information of Licensee, and all right, title and interest in and to such Reports and information shall be owned by Licensee.
perturbation.changed_text: The Reports and all information contained in the Reports shall be considered Confidential Information of both Licensee and Licensor, and all right, title and interest in and to such Reports and information shall be jointly owned by Licensee and Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Reports are considered Confidential Information solely of Licensee and all rights are owned by Licensee. The modified text now states that Reports are considered Confidential Information of both Licensee and Licensor, and all rights are jointly owned by Licensee and Licensor. This change contradicts the original provision that gives exclusive ownership and confidentiality to Licensee and now introduces shared ownership and confidentiality. Section 8 (b) which initially says that only Licensee has Confidential information is now directly contradicted, since now this section is stating that the licensor also shares Confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee will provide Licensor with reports ("Reports") containing statistical information collected by Licensee on (i) Users' use of the Titles, (ii) distribution channels used by Licensee for the distribution of the Titles, (iii) sub-licensees to which the Titles were sub-distributed by Licensee and (if permitted under Licensee's agreements with the sublicensees) any relevant reports received by Licensee from those sublicensees, and (iv) any other information that the Licensor may request Licensee to gather from time to time, subject to mutual approval. The Reports will be delivered in a format that is mutually agreed upon by the parties. The Reports and all information contained in the Reports shall be considered Confidential Information of Licensee, and all right, title and interest in and to such Reports and information shall be owned by Licensee.
perturbation.justification: The original text assigns exclusive ownership and confidentiality of the Reports to the Licensee, as stated in the contradicted text: "The Reports and all information contained in the Reports shall be considered Confidential Information of Licensee, and all right, title and interest in and to such Reports and information shall be owned by Licensee." The changed text contradicts this by stating that the Reports and information are confidential to both Licensor and Licensee, and rights are jointly owned. This directly conflicts with the original allocation of exclusive rights to the Licensee, creating an in-text contradiction. This is an instance of a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction, as per the definition provided.
